chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1822 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For orders on MA-16026/2016 2. For katcha peshi. 30.11.2016. Mr. Rao Faisal Ali, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Sher Muhammad Leghari, State Counsel a/w SIP Saleh Ram SHO P.S Badar Farm District Umerkot and ASI Sher Muhammad o/b of SHO P.S Umerkot City. = Mr. Muhammad Hashim M. Leghari, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.5 to 12 and submits that between the parties, contentious litigation is sub judice before different Courts. However, the private respondents are not causing any harassment to the petitioner. This has been seriously disputed by Mr. Rao Faisal Ali, the learned Counsel representing the petitioner and he states that constantly the petitioner is receiving threats from different persons that he will be dispossessed from their properties. Be that as it may, it is expected that both sides will act in a civilized manner and will seek redressal of their grievances through due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. It is also regretfully noted that petitioner and respondent No.5 are real brothers but they have filed criminal cases against each other. In this regard, both the learned Counsel should make sincere efforts for bringing about an amicable settlement amongst the parties. This petition is disposed of in view of the above observations and with directions that respondents are directed to desist from causing any harassment to the petitioner and if they have any genuine grievance against the petitioner, the same will only be redressed through due process of law.

In this regard, both the learned Counsel should make sincere efforts for bringing about an amicable settlement amongst the parties. This petition is disposed of in view of the above observations and with directions that respondents are directed to desist from causing any harassment to the petitioner and if they have any genuine grievance against the petitioner, the same will only be redressed through due process of law. It is also expected that petitioners will also not take undue advantage of this order by causing any harassment to respondent No.5. JUDGE Shahid
